segments.title: Crust, Blueberry Layer & Crumble Topping
steps: Preheat oven to ['temp']{160}. Wrap a 6-inch springform pan tightly with aluminium foil. Line the bottom with parchment paper.
Crush ['ing']{Digestive biscuits} into fine crumbs. Mix with ['ing']{Brown sugar} and ['ing']{Cinnamon}. Add melted ['ing']{Unsalted butter} until combined. Press firmly into the pan's bottom.
Bake for 10 minutes. Cool. Reduce oven to ['temp']{150}.
(Blueberry Layer) Combine ['ing']{Fresh blueberries}, ['ing']{Granulated sugar}, and ['ing']{Lemon juice} in a skillet. Cook over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until berries soften and release juice.
Sprinkle 1 tsp ['ing']{All-purpose flour} over them and cook for 1 more minute, stirring, until thickened. Spread on a plate to cool completely.
(Crumble Topping) Mix ['ing']{All-purpose flour}, ['ing']{Dark brown sugar}, and ['ing']{Quick oats}. Rub in the cold ['ing']{Unsalted butter}, cubed, with your fingers until crumbly. Refrigerate.

segments.title: Cheesecake Filling & Assembly
steps: Beat ['ing']{Cream cheese} until smooth. Add ['ing']{Granulated sugar} and ['ing']{Lemon zest}; beat until combined. Mix in ['ing']{Sour cream}, ['ing']{Cornstarch}, and ['ing']{Vanilla extract}. Add the ['ing']{Large egg} and ['ing']{Egg yolk}, mixing on low until just combined.
Pour ⅔ of the filling over the crust. Dollop the cooled blueberries over the top. Cover with remaining filling. Sprinkle the chilled crumble evenly over everything.

Place the foil-wrapped pan into a roasting pan. Place in the oven, then carefully pour hot water into the roasting pan until it comes halfway up the cheesecake pan. Bake for 55-70 minutes, until the edges are set but the center jiggles slightly.
Turn the oven off. Crack the door open and let the cheesecake cool inside for 1 hour. Remove from the water bath and run a knife around the edge. Cool completely on a rack.
Refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight before serving.
